items.title: Selangor Sultan backs King on Rukun Negara decree
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, backed His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, in his decree against those who reject the principles of the Rukun Negara. In conjunction with the King's official birthday, Sultan Sharafuddin and the Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, extended their congratulations to His Majesty today. Sultan Sharafuddin said the people, especially the younger generation, must understand, appreciate and practise the values enshrined in the Rukun Negara to build a harmonious and peaceful society among Malaysia's multi-ethnic population.

items.title: Op Tiris: Cops seize five vehicles believed modified with extra fuel tanks
items.summary: KANGAR: Police have seized five vehicles suspected of being modified with additional fuel tanks concealed beneath the rear seats at separate locations around the Padang Besar district. Padang Besar police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Shokri Abdullah said the seizure of one pickup truck and four sedan cars was carried out under Op Tiris 4.0 between April 22 until today. He said a local woman, two local men and two foreign men aged between 30 and 55 were detained during the operation.

items.title: France seizes Russia-linked oil tanker with ties to Iranian magnate
items.summary: BREST, France: France said on Monday it had detained a Russia-linked oil tanker in the Atlantic, with the Kremlin comparing the latest seizure aimed at tackling Moscow's sanctions-busting shadow fleet to "international piracy." Since September, France has boarded three other ships believed to belong to the shadow fleet, used by Russia to circumvent Western sanctions on its fossil fuel exports imposed over the Kremlin's 2022 invasion of Ukraine. They border on international piracy," said President Vladimir Putin's spokesman, Dmitry Peskov.

items.title: One missing, six saved after boat capsizes in Kapit
items.summary: SIBU: A man is feared drowned after the boat he was on capsized in Sungai Gaat, Kapit, near SK Nanga Balang yesterday evening. A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said the victim, Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, 32, is missing while six other passengers who were on the boat managed to save themselves. "The six individuals managed to save themselves while the victim was seen drifting about 50m from where the boat capsized before disappearing from view," the spokesman said in a statement today, adding that the victim has yet to be found.

items.title: Johor Amanah confident voters will back state govt aligned with Putrajaya
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Amanah has signalled its readiness for the state election, declaring that the Pakatan Harapan (PH) machinery has already been activated following the dissolution of the state assembly today. Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said Johor PH was acting in line with resolutions made at the recent PH convention, where preparations for the state polls had already been set in motion. "Johor PH, as stated at the recent convention, has already mobilised its machinery and is prepared to face the state election," he said.

items.title: EC to discuss key dates for Johor polls after official notification of dissolution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission will hold a special meeting for the 16th Johor election as soon as it receives official notification from the speaker of the assembly on the date of the dissolution. EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the special meeting would discuss the key dates for the state polls. "The EC takes note of the press conference by Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi regarding the dissolution of the 15th Johor legislative assembly (today).

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - June 1, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: HERE are today's biggest stories: King urges restraint His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has reminded political leaders to exercise caution in their public statements and uphold proper conduct. Johor polls loom The Johor state assembly has been dissolved, setting the stage for a state election within the next 60 days. Ee Wei injured Mixed doubles world champion Toh Ee Wei has suffered a partial ACL tear, putting her World Championships title defence in doubt.

items.title: Johor election: Stay clear of provocation, divisive politics, Samsuri tells PN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar has called on the coalition's leadership, machinery and party members to stay clear of provocation, hate politics and divisive tactics during the Johor election. He said PN would enter the election arena with responsibility, maturity and professionalism, with the interests of the people as its priority. "I hope the entire machinery can avoid any form of extreme provocation, hate politics and divisiveness, and instead present a calmer, fact-based and solution-oriented politics," he said in a statement.

items.title: Health Ministry moves to block 4 online vape shops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry has submitted requests to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to block four websites for online advertising and sale of vape products. In a statement, it said that the four websites are part of 10 sites suspected of violating the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act. The Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act came into force on Oct 1, 2024, with full enforcement beginning Oct 1 last year.

items.title: Election fever builds in Johor as Khaled Nordin steps up ground engagement
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Election fever is already rippling through Johor's political landscape just hours after the announcement of the dissolution of the state assembly. Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin, who is Kota Tinggi member of parliament, is among those expected to intensify ground outreach at his constituency as political activity begins to shift into pre-campaign mode. The state assembly was dissolved today, triggering preparations for the next state election and effectively resetting the political board in Johor.

items.title: Maharani Freeport group exec chairman conferred 'Tan Sri' title
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maharani Freeport Sdn Bhd group executive chairman Tan Sri Dr Daing A Malek Daing A Rahaman has been conferred the Darjah Kebesaran Panglima Mangku Negara (PMN), which carries the title "Tan Sri", by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. An advisory member of the Johor Council of Royal Court, Daing A Malek, who is Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd executive director, is also the executive chairman of Maharani Freeport's parent entity, Maharani Energy Gateway Sdn Bhd. He is the founder of Astaka Holdings Ltd, founded in 1993, and has held executive chairman positions at Astaka Padu Sdn Bhd, a chairman position at SIPP Energy Sdn Bhd, and a director position at Seaview Holdings Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Defence minister: Norway needs to be held responsible for missile export cancellation, Malaysia’s right to ask for compensation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Malaysia has the right to claim appropriate compensation from Norway and the company involved, following the cancellation of the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) export licence, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He said the claim is justified given that Malaysia has suffered financial losses, as well as lost valuable time and opportunity as a result of the cancellation. If Norway had informed us earlier about the export licence cancellation, those funds could have been redirected to other purposes,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: BN-led Johor govt has a proven track record, says Wee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong said that the Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) government proved that political stability, effective governance, and a clear development agenda could yield beneficial results for the people. He said this following the dissolution of the Johor state assembly today, adding that it will pave the way for a fresh mandate from the people. "The dissolution of the state assembly marks another significant chapter in Johor's democratic journey," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Johor Pakatan says prepared for state polls following dissolution of state assembly
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) is prepared to face the upcoming state election following the dissolution of the state legislative assembly. In a joint statement, Johor PH said the coalition had expected that Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) would dissolve the assembly soon to pave way for an election. The statement was co-signed by Johor PH chairman and Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) chief Aminolhuda Hassan; Johor PKR chief Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa; and Johor DAP chief Teo Nie Ching.

items.title: Explosion kills 5 at South Korean aerospace plant
items.summary: SEOUL: An explosion at a plant run by South Korean defence firm Hanwha Aerospace killed five people on Monday, local fire authorities said. The explosion was reported to authorities at around 11:00 am (0200 GMT) at Hanwha's plant in Daejeon, about 150 kilometres south of Seoul. Hanwha Aerospace is a major South Korean defence manufacturer which produces weapons, artillery systems and aerospace components.

items.title: Hassan Karim: Hold Johor polls and GE16 together for political stability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pasir Gudang member of parliament Hassan Abdul Karim has welcomed the dissolution of the Johor assembly as a move that will reduce political uncertainty. However, for the same reason, he said Parliament should be dissolved as well to pave the way for the 16th General Election (GE16) to be held simultaneously with the Johor Election. "The dissolution of the Johor assembly helps reduce tension and political guesswork — at least in Johor there is now certainty.

items.title: Malaysia has the right to demand compensation from Norway, says Khaled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is actively pursuing alternative solutions to meet its defence requirements following the cancellation of the naval strike missile (NSM) procurement, but will continue seeking accountability and compensation from Norway over the failed deal. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said moving on from the cancelled procurement did not mean accepting what had happened without consequences. "Yes, Malaysia is moving on.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain alert issued for six states until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect several areas in six states until 9 pm today. Meanwhile, in Sabah, the warning covers the Interior division (Nabawan) and Sandakan division (Telupid, Beluran and Sandakan). In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Betong), Sibu (Kanowit), Kapit (Song and Bukit Mabong), Miri (Beluru and Telang Usan), and Limbang.

items.title: I'm not jumping ship, says DAP's Marina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP assemblyman Marina Ibrahim has denied claims that she is defecting or has been "bought over" following her decision not to defend her Skudai seat in the upcoming Johor state election. "Some have said, 'Are you jumping parties?' "Many people think Sun Tzu teaches how to defeat enemies in war.

items.title: PM Anwar expected at Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebration on June 21
items.summary: KUCHING, June 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to attend this year’s Sarawak state-level Gawai Day celebrations to be held here on June 21, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He added that the Yang Dipertua Negeri of Sarawak Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar will be the guest of honour at the event, which is also expected to be attended by Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadilah Yusof. He told reporters this at the 2026 Gawai Dayak Day Open House at the Unit for Other Religions (UNIFOR) Complex here today.

items.title: 'No easy fight, but we are ready,' says PH as Johor heads to polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan said it was fully prepared for the state's 16th election after the legislative assembly was dissolved today. State Amanah and PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan, Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa and state DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching said they had anticipated that Barisan Nasional would seek a fresh mandate in the near future and maintained that preparations for the election had been underway. [[nid:1453128]] "From the beginning, PH anticipated that BN would dissolve the state assembly in the near future.

items.title: Former MAG chief Izham Ismail joins Philippine Airlines board
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) managing director Datuk Captain Izham Ismail has been appointed as an independent director of Philippine Airlines (PAL), marking his latest move in the aviation industry following his retirement from MAG earlier this year. According to the Philippine-based business publication insider Insider PH, PAL has appointed Izham and fuel industry veteran Edgar Chua to its board as independent directors. Business Times has contacted PAL for comment.

items.title: Pas urges simultaneous elections in other states, GE16 alongside Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas has called for the dissolution of Parliament and other state assemblies so that elections can be held simultaneously with Johor. Its deputy president, Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, said polls in Melaka, Negri Sembilan, Sarawak and Perlis should be held at the same time as the Johor Election so that time and resources can be saved. Tuan Ibrahim said Pas would convene an emergency meeting to discuss the Johor Election, and that Perikatan Nasional was also expected to hold a meeting in the near future.

items.title: Malaysian troops complete multinational military exercise in India
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian troops enhanced their interoperability with regional forces during a multinational military exercise hosted by the Indian army in Meghalaya, India. The exercise, held at Umroi Military Station in Meghalaya from May 18 to 31, concluded with a 72-hour validation exercise that demonstrated the interoperability, mutual trust and collective readiness of participating armed forces in addressing common security challenges. [[nid:1453106]] It said mixed teams comprising personnel from different countries trained together throughout the exercise, enhancing interoperability, capacity building and mutual trust among the participating forces.

items.title: Police on the trail of three suspects involved in armed robbery cases in Pekan Gurney
items.summary: IPOH, June 1 — Police are hunting for three men, believed to be Indonesian nationals, involved in a gang robbery armed with machetes at a house in the Pekan Gurney area, Manjung yesterday. Manjung District Police Chief, ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman, said that his department received a report regarding the incident from a female victim in her 40s at 6.27am. “In the incident, three male suspects, believed to be Indonesian nationals, entered the house and threatened the victim with a machete before fleeing with cash and jewelry.

items.title: Saudi award reflects strength of Malaysia's haj management system
items.summary: SEPANG: Malaysia's haj operation this year was highly organised, systematic and well managed, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. He said the prestigious Labaytum Diamond Award 2026 awarded to Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) was proof that Malaysia has one of the best haj management systems in the world. Zulkifli said the Labaytum Diamond Award is the highest recognition presented by the Saudi Haj and Umrah Ministry to countries that demonstrate excellence in haj management and services.

items.title: After the AI binge, companies balk at soaring bills
items.summary: Playing by a well-worn Silicon Valley playbook, AI companies charged rock-bottom prices to hook customers after ChatGPT burst onto the scene. Prices are rising across the board, and one big reason is AI agents. Those charges are measured in tokens – the basic unit AI companies use to bill customers.

items.title: Philippine senator Estrada surrenders over graft case
items.summary: MANILA: A Philippine senator who is the son of a former president surrendered to police on Monday after a court ordered his arrest over a charge that he received illicit payouts as part of an infrastructure scandal that sparked public protests last year. The anti-graft court ordered the arrest of Senator Jose "Jinggoy" Estrada for plunder, a non-bailable offence under Philippine laws. The Office of the Ombudsman last week charged Estrada with violating anti-corruption laws for earning "kickbacks" amounting to 573 million pesos (US$9.3 million).

items.title: Johor DAP: Marina Ibrahim not offered GLC post, but position with statutory body involved in welfare
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — Johor DAP has clarified that the party had offered to appoint incumbent Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim to helm a statutory body and not a government-linked company (GLC) as seen in a letter that went viral. Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali said there is a difference in a statutory body and a GLC. He said Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching had stated that she offered Marina to lead a statutory body and not a GLC.

items.title: TTDI residents unimpressed with park upgrade, want transparency over plans, outstanding works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Visitors to the Bukit Kiara Federal Park today were disappointed after a near two-year closure only resulted in visible enhancements about 300m from the entrance. Residents of Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI) have voiced concerns about the RM6 million channelled into upgrading the park, calling for transparency on the original promised scope and a timeline for completing outstanding works. Park visitor and TTDI resident Eugene Yong, 45, said the public started entering the park one to two weeks ago with construction debris still scattered around the park.

items.title: Iranian attacks damaged 20 US military sites since war began
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Iranian strikes have damaged 20 US military sites since the start of the US-Israeli war against Teheran in February, a BBC analysis of satellite images and videos showed Monday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. The satellite image analysis also indicated that Iranian strikes severely damaged US refuelling and surveillance aircraft at Prince Sultan Airbase in Saudi Arabia, with wrecked aircraft and burn marks visible at the site. Elsewhere, Iranian strikes also hit Ali Al Salem Airbase and Camp Arifjan in Kuwait.

items.title: Gold falls as renewed Middle East tensions fuel inflation fears
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold prices fell on Monday as renewed US-Iran tensions pushed the dollar and oil prices higher, fuelling fears of inflation and reinforcing the higher-for-longer interest rate outlook. Spot gold was down 0.8 per cent at US$4,498.89 per ounce at 0909 GMT after hitting a two-week high on Friday. US gold futures for August delivery fell 1.4 per cent to US$4,528.90.

items.title: Five hurt in five‑vehicle collision in Kudat
items.summary: KUDAT, June 1 — Five people were injured in a road accident involving five vehicles at Jalan Kampung Kandawayon here late yesterday. Kudat Fire and Rescue Station chief Darikson Galanang said the crash involved a Perodua Axia, a Proton, a Toyota Revo and two Perodua Myvi cars. Two other injured victims, aged 24 and 26, were the driver and passenger of a grey Myvi.

items.title: Johor state assembly dissolved, Onn Hafiz cites need for mandate to drive development
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 1 — The Johor state legislative assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the state’s upcoming election. Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the dissolution after obtaining the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail Sultan Ibrahim earlier today. The previous Johor state election was held in March 2022.

items.title: Man missing, feared drowned after boat overturns near SK Nanga Balang in Kapit
items.summary: KANOWIT, June 1 — A 32-year-old man is feared drowned after a boat carrying seven passengers capsized near SK Nanga Balang in Gaat, Kapit, this morning. The victim, identified as Spielberg Dylan Sekunyir, was reported missing following the incident, while six other passengers managed to reach safety. A spokesperson for the department said firefighters from the Kapit Fire and Rescue Station (BBP Kapit) were dispatched to the scene after first proceeding to the Kapit police station to obtain information on the incident.

items.title: China accuses Japan defence minister of 'baseless' claims
items.summary: BEIJING: China accused Japan's defence minister on Monday of spreading "baseless" claims and sowing confusion, a day after he took a veiled swipe at Beijing. Defence Minister Shinjiro Koizumi had pledged on Sunday to keep strengthening Japan's military and warned China was expanding its capabilities without sufficient transparency. China's foreign ministry said on Monday that Koizumi's comments were "entirely baseless."

items.title: Hamzah allies back Tuan Ibrahim's push for Muafakat Nasional revival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two allies of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, both of whom were sacked from Bersatu, have backed Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man's proposal to revive Muafakat Nasional (MN). Former Bersatu supreme council member Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah welcomed the proposal, saying it was consistent with Hamzah's vision of building a grand political coalition ahead of the 16th General Election (GE16). The Indera Mahkota member of Parliament said Hamzah had unveiled the "New National Consensus" framework on June 22 last year, aimed at making Malaysia a more advanced, prosperous and harmonious nation.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolved, election within 60 days [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor state assembly has been dissolved, paving the way for the next state election to be held within 60 days. "The 56-seat Johor state assembly is officially dissolved today, paving the way for the 16th Johor state election," he said during a special press conference at his official residence in Saujana this afternoon. Also present were former Johor executive council members and Johor Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi, among others.

items.title: Auto dealers expect new hire-purchase rules to boost buyer interest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The implementation of the reducing balance method and effective interest rate system under the Hire-Purchase (Amendment) Act 2026 is expected to boost business, say second-hand car dealers and potential buyers. Car salesman Aviss Thung said the new method, where interest is calculated based on the outstanding principal instead of the original loan amount, would make financing easier to explain to customers. But once they return and understand the new rules, I expect more interest in car purchases," he said.

items.title: Public rescue injured driver, passenger after crash near SMK Sematan, says Sarawak Fire Dept
items.summary: KUCHING, June 1 — A man was seriously injured in a road accident involving a sedan in front of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sematan yesterday afternoon. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) Operations Centre said the injured man was the driver of the vehicle involved in the accident. The male driver was not trapped but sustained serious injuries, while a female passenger also suffered injuries.

items.title: Family among four killed in Toyota Vios as BMW, Mercedes, Wira and Alza collide near Simpang Renggam Prison
items.summary: KLUANG, June 1 — Four people were killed and two others injured in a collision involving five vehicles along Jalan Renggam–Simpang Renggam, in front of Simpang Renggam Prison, this afternoon. The crash, which occurred at about 1pm, involved a Perodua Alza, Proton Wira, Mercedes Benz, BMW and Toyota Vios, Harian Metro reported. Four occupants of the Mercedes Benz also survived: Mohd Azhar Ahmad, 63; his son Muhammad Hariz Mohd Azhar, 23; Rahimar A Rahman, 57; and a female passenger whose identity is still unknown.

items.title: Fahmi: Malaysians must heed King’s call to uphold Rukun Negara, preserve unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Politicians and Malaysians in general should heed and uphold the call by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for the people to embrace and practise the spirit and values of the Rukun Negara in their daily lives, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. “I respectfully uphold His Majesty’s call, and I believe politicians across the country will do the same. Earlier, Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, graced the ceremony.

items.title: Johor assembly dissolution speculation mounts as Onn Hafiz set to hold 5pm press conference
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — All eyes are on Johor today amid speculation that the state assembly could be dissolved. According to the New Straits Times , Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is scheduled to hold a special press conference at his official residence at 5pm. The announcement, eagerly awaited across the political divide, is expected to pave the way for state polls.

items.title: Fahmi: Social media platforms must respect Malaysian laws as age‑verification begins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Social media platforms operating in Malaysia must respect and comply with the country’s laws, particularly those relating to online safety and the protection of children, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the reminder comes as the government begins enforcing age-verification requirements for social media account registration from today through the implementation of the Child Protection Code (CPC) and Risk Mitigation Code (RMC). Fahmi said the two codes, introduced under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), represent a crucial first step in protecting individuals under the age of 16 from online harm.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues alert for thunderstorms, heavy rain in seven states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit seven states until 5pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). In a weather warning issued at 3.15 pm, MetMalaysia said the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, and Kuala Kangsar. In Selangor, the warning covers Hulu Langat and Sepang, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.

items.title: BN at 52: Zahid Hamidi rallies coalition for unity, renewal and resilience ahead polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for mature politics, responsible leadership and practical solutions to address current challenges in an increasingly complex global environment. In conjunction with BN’s 52nd anniversary, Ahmad Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, said the coalition must continue to move forward with a spirit of renewal, grounded in experience and an openness to adapt to future demands. Ahmad Zahid, who is Umno president, said BN was formed 52 years ago out of a spirit of understanding, moderation and cooperation among Malaysia’s diverse communities, to build the nation collectively.

items.title: Kelantan JPJ: BMW 320i confiscated over fake road tax, five speeding summonses uncovered
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 1 — The Kelantan Road Transport Department (JPJ) seized a luxury BMW 320i on suspicion of displaying a fake road tax document during a special motorcycle operation held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha Operations (HRAA) yesterday. Kelantan JPJ director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said that checks using the Smart Enforcement Device (SmED) found that the vehicle’s road tax had actually expired in July 2025, while the fake copy indicated that it was valid until August 2026. “The vehicle was seized under Section 108 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for allegedly displaying a forged document,” he told reporters after the HRAA 2026 Special Motorcycle Operation at the Malaysian Road Transport Academy (Eastern Region) here.

items.title: GOF joins helicopter‑deployed teams in Gunung Batu Putih search for Jaslinda Saludin missing May 23
items.summary: He said the GOF personnel, together with eight members of JBPM’s Special Tactical Operation and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM), eight Forestry Department personnel and mountain guides, were flown into the search area aboard an MI-17 helicopter, significantly reducing travel time compared with previous overland routes through Gunung Batu Bujang and Pos Kuala Woh. Meanwhile, Perak JBPM director Datuk Sayani Saidon said SAR efforts remain focused on the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu, involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, mountain guides and the GOF. He said SAR operations were continuing in the area between Kem Kantoi and Gunung Rayu involving personnel from JBPM, the Forestry Department, Forestry Mountain Guides and the GOF.

items.title: Nine‑year‑old boy dies after fall from 13th‑floor apartment in Ampang, police probe negligence
items.summary: Ampang Jaya police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said they received information regarding the incident from a woman at about 7.45pm after she found the victim unconscious. He said the boy was confirmed dead at the scene by an assistant medical officer from the Ampang Hospital. Following the incident, Khairul Anuar said police have recorded statements from five individuals, comprising the boy’s parents and family members as well as witnesses.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi demands DAP disclosure of GLC posts as Marina Ibrahim’s retirement sparks political uproar
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi has accused DAP of using government‑linked company (GLC) positions as political bargaining chips, following controversy over a purported offer involving Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim. Puad claimed the matter came to light when Marina allegedly rejected a proposal to contest the Tiram state seat in exchange for a GLC post. “DAP is worse.

items.title: Skudai DAP rep Marina Ibrahim hits back at ‘bought over’ rumours amid purported GLC offer
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, June 1 — Skudai assemblyman Marina Ibrahim today rejected claims she was defecting or had been “bought over”, saying her political decisions were made after consulting her closest supporters and stressing her focus was on solving problems rather than constant confrontation. In a lengthy Facebook post, Marina invoked Sun Tzu’s Art of War to defend her non-confrontational style, arguing that “the best victory is one achieved without fighting”. In a Berita Harian report, Marina said she had shared “the letter” in a Johor DAP WhatsApp group.

items.title: Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals online through MyTax and track status in real time, says IRB
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 1 — Taxpayers can now file assessment appeals or apply for an extension of time to appeal tax assessments raised under the Income Tax Act 1967 through the new e-Assessment Appeal service on the MyTax portal. “Among the new experiences for taxpayers include the ability to submit assessment appeals or applications for extension of time from anywhere and in real time,” the IRB said. For access, the IRB said individual taxpayers may submit appeals or extension applications via the individual taxpayer role on MyTax.

items.title: Melaka police: Viral video edited, no escort of foreign nationals in Tanjung Minyak
items.summary: MELAKA, June 1 — Police have denied claims that they were escorting several vehicles allegedly carrying foreign nationals in Tanjung Minyak here. Acting Melaka Tengah police chief Supt Halim Abas said the viral video in question was believed to have been edited by combining footage from two different locations. He said checks found that the footage showing a police four-wheel-drive vehicle exiting a junction was indeed recorded near Masjid Tun Mohd Khalil Yaakub in Tanjung Minyak.

items.title: IT consultant loses nearly RM1.5m after installing fake ‘Kakaue’ crypto investment app
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 1 — An IT consultant has lost nearly RM1.5 million after falling victim to an online cryptocurrency investment scam syndicate known as ‘Crypto Kakaue’. Azizee said the victim subsequently joined a WhatsApp group chat called ‘Saham Mececah Hati Orang’, where he was told about the investment and offered high returns. He said the victim was told to install an app called ‘Kakaue’ and conduct cryptocurrency transactions.

items.title: Police arrest two women after Myanmar man found stabbed to death in Simpang Ampat house
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, June 1 — Police have detained two women to assist in investigations into the murder of a Myanmar national at a house in Taman Belibis, Simpang Ampat here yesterday, bringing the total number of suspects arrested to three. Seberang Perai Selatan (SPS) district police chief Supt Jay January Siowou said the arrests followed the detention of the main suspect, a 38-year-old man who was apprehended by members of the public at the scene before being handed over to the police yesterday. He said preliminary investigations found that the incident is believed to have stemmed from a domestic dispute.

items.title: On RMAF’s 68th anniversary, defence minister says Mindef pledges to boost force’s capabilities and readiness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) remains committed to strengthening the capabilities and readiness of the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) through the development of assets, technology and human capital to ensure the force remains at the forefront of defending the country’s airspace. The history of the RMAF began when the Air Force Ordinance was passed by Parliament on June 2, 1958, with the force initially known as the Royal Federal Air Force. Following the formation of Malaysia on Sept 16, 1963, the force was subsequently renamed the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF).

items.title: Sabah detains 300 in Likas raid against deviant religious group banned by state fatwa council
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 1 — Some 300 individuals, including 12 key leaders and organisers of a deviant religious group, were detained in a joint enforcement operation conducted by the Sabah Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAINS) and the police in Likas here today. Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said the operation was carried out following a public complaint received by JHEAINS on May 28 regarding the ‘Jalsa Salana Wilayah Sabah Ke-32’ programme held from May 29 to 31. He said checks found that the programme was linked to the Ahmadiyah Qadiani movement, which was declared deviant and contrary to Islamic teachings by the Sabah State Fatwa Council and gazetted in 2007.

items.title: King confers posthumous award on Survey and Mapping Dept officer who died on duty at Malaysia–Brunei border
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — A Malaysian Survey and Mapping Department (JUPEM) personnel, Donysius Gabriel Goh, who died while on duty at the Malaysia–Brunei border in March this year, has been posthumously awarded the Bintang Jasa Perkasa Persekutuan (JPP) in recognition of his service and contributions. According to the official list of recipients for the 2026 federal awards, medals and honours in conjunction with the Official Birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, the late Donysius is the sole recipient of the honour. A total of 161 individuals were conferred federal honours in conjunction with this year’s official birthday celebration of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.

items.title: Ministers extend congratulations, prayers to King on official birthday
items.summary: Meanwhile, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said prayed that His Majesty would be blessed with divine grace to continue safeguarding the people with wisdom, dignity and compassion for the prosperity and well-being of the nation. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, likewise, extended his highest congratulations and heartfelt loyalty to His Majesty in conjunction with the historic occasion. Congratulatory messages were also conveyed by Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek; Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin; and Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali.

items.title: DPMs Fadillah, Zahid convey congratulations to King on official birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 1 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof has extended his congratulations to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, on the occasion of the King’s Official Birthday. “May the King continue to reign with majesty, sovereignty and honour, guiding the nation and its people with wisdom, fairness and prudence,” according to the post. Meanwhile, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi also conveyed his highest congratulations and royal greetings to the King in conjunction with His Majesty’s Official Birthday.
